title: eriogonum kennedyi subsp. gracilipes
intro: Eriogonum kennedyi subsp. gracilipes, commonly known as Kennedy's buckwheat, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the buckwheat family. This shrubby perennial is native to the southwestern United States and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The plant's leaves, stems, and roots are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making it beneficial for improving skin health, reducing pain, and aiding in the healing of injuries. Kennedy's buckwheat can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or salve, and is also used topically as a poultice to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
